column2: In mobile liquid-damage triage, LCI stands for {{c1::Liquid Contact Indicator}}.
column3: An LCI can support the damage history, but it does not make the device safe to power on.
column4:
Why

This compact acronym recall supports escalation notes and liquid-damage inspection without turning the card into a broad procedure.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.53; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.4
column9: A+::220-1201::5.4 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingMobileDevices Cloze HighYield LiquidDamage Memory Source::Messer-v170

column2: The mobile touchscreen input layer is commonly called the {{c1::digitizer}}.
column3: Digitizer symptoms are input symptoms, not automatically display-output symptoms.
column4:
Memory Trick

The digitizer turns physical touch into digital input. Keep that separate from display brightness or image-quality problems.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.53; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.4
column9: A+::220-1201::5.4 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingMobileDevices Cloze Touchscreen Memory Source::Messer-v170

column2: Random mobile cursor or touch movement without user input is called {{c1::cursor drift}}.
column3: If the device supports it, touch calibration can be a targeted troubleshooting step.
column4:
Why

The term helps connect the symptom to the calibration decision card without requiring a broad repair answer.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.54; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.4
column9: A+::220-1201::5.4 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingMobileDevices Cloze Touchscreen Calibration Memory Source::Messer-v170
